libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. For deterministic tests, prefer ConfigLoaderBuilder.empty() with explicit default decoders/param mappers and MapPropertySource or built-in .props/.properties parsing. Avoid YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON examples unless intentionally adding the corresponding optional hoplite-* format module, because hoplite-core alone only has Java properties parsing built in.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Hoplite documentation's typical YAML/JSON/HOCON examples require optional format modules, but those are not necessary to exercise hoplite-core itself.
Hoplite 3 applies EnvironmentVariablesPropertySource by default; tests using ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() may be sensitive to CI environment variables unless they isolate property sources.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful hoplite-core coverage is reachable with the resolved artifact and normal Kotlin/JUnit scaffold; core includes built-in properties parsing, MapPropertySource, default decoders, preprocessors, and property sources, and its transitive dependencies are declared by the artifact.
